data.title: Tungsten Automation adds three to executive team to boost AI innovation: Danielle Weinblatt Chief Product Officer, Adam Field Chief AI Officer, Ryan Hubbard EVP R&D.
data.description: Tungsten Automation Corporation, a top player in AI-driven document and workflow automation based in Irvine, California, unveiled significant changes to its executive team. The company welcomed Danielle Weinblatt as Chief Product Officer, Adam Field as Chief AI Officer, and Ryan Hubbard as Executive Vice President and Head of Research & Development. These appointments highlight the company's dedication to enhancing its AI-centered product strategy and bringing cutting-edge innovation into the arena.

data.title: Dark Horse CPAs hires Heather Huotari as Associate Principal to build tech-forward SMB accounting practice. Jan 20, 2026.
data.description: Heather Huotari, CPA, has recently joined Dark Horse CPAs in Cardiff by the Sea, California on January 20, 2026. Dark Horse is a leading accounting and tax firm catering to small businesses nationwide. Huotari's addition as an Associate demonstrates their commitment to building a tech-savvy SMB accounting practice. This move underlines Dark Horse's focus on expanding their team with talented professionals as they continue to provide services to a growing number of small businesses in the country.

data.title: Dow Jones index plunges 650 points due to Trump's Greenland tariff threats; Nvidia among big losers. Gold stocks rise.
data.description: On Tuesday, the Dow Jones index plummeted by 650 points due to President Trump's threats of imposing tariffs on Greenland. Within the Dow, Nvidia experienced significant losses. The latest stock market update reports on the mounting losses for the Dow Jones, with live coverage highlighting the rise of gold stocks amidst the turmoil.

data.title: Bitcoin's Fear & Greed Index flashes "golden cross", with 30-day moving average above 90-day for first time since May 2025 - potentially bullish signal.
data.description: Bitcoin (BTC) is currently hovering around $90,000, with sentiment indicators indicating a potentially bullish signal. The Fear & Greed Index for Bitcoin has shown a technical crossover, with the 30-day moving average surpassing the 90-day average for the first time since May 2025. This "golden cross" suggests an improvement in short-term sentiment compared to the overall trend, according to CryptoQuant data. Typically, this signal emerges after extended periods of fear, when doubts are still high. Analysts, however, remain cautious following a significant rejection at higher price levels.
Dmitriev's meeting with Witkoff and Kushner in Davos lasted more than two hours - reports

data.title: Lightpath wins third major network award in Columbus, adding 304 miles to network; expansion driven by AI demand.
data.description: Lightpath, a leading company in the telecommunications industry, recently obtained its third significant network award in the Greater Columbus market. This award has expanded the Columbus network to an impressive total of 304 miles. The achievement underscores Lightpath's commitment to investing in response to the increasing demand for advanced connectivity solutions, particularly those tailored to support hyperscale requirements for artificial intelligence applications. This accomplishment marks a significant milestone for Lightpath and demonstrates their dedication to meeting the evolving needs of their customers.

data.title: KBRA rates COOPR 2026-CES1's $253.3M RMBS transaction with 99.9% CES. Originated by Mr. Cooper, it is seasoned one month.
data.description: In New York, credit rating agency KBRA has assigned preliminary ratings to eight classes of Certificates from COOPR Residential Mortgage Trust 2026-CES1. The RMBS transaction totals $253.3 million and is sponsored by Nationstar Mortgage LLC d/b/a Mr. Cooper and Loan Funding Structure VIII LLC. The pool consists primarily of 3,438 newly originated closed-end second lien mortgages, accounting for 99.9% of the total. All loans in the pool have a seasoning of one month and are originated by Mr. Cooper.

data.title: HII's mission technologies unit secures spot in $25.4B Pentagon defense tech contract alongside 11 others.
data.description: Huntington Ingalls Industries, Inc. disclosed that its Mission Technologies sector secured a position in a significant defense microelectronics contract. This division is among the selected 12 firms for the 10-year, $25.4 billion Advanced Technology Support Program V contract. This multi-award Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract aims to offer engineering solutions for electronic and software issues within the U.S. Department of Defense and various federal agencies. The program's primary goal is to swiftly provide federal clients with cutting-edge technologies and support.

data.title: Stocks tumble as Treasury yields hit 5-month highs after Trump's aggressive trade stance towards Europe. S&P 500 falls 1.5% to 6,840.
data.description: Wall Street experienced a significant drop on Tuesday following President Trump's aggressive trade stance towards Europe after the Martin Luther King Jr. Day holiday. The S&P 500 fell 1.5% to 6,840, marking its worst session since November, with consumer discretionary and technology stocks leading the decline. The Nasdaq 100 dropped 1.4%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average lost 1.3%, and the Russell 2000 eased 0.8%. Trump threatened European countries with 10% tariffs starting Feb. 1, potentially increasing to 25% by June if Greenland control negotiations fail. European officials warned of reciprocal measures affecting up to 25% of U.S. exports to Europe, with possible reductions in Treasury holdings. Trump also mentioned imposing 200% tariffs on French wines and champagne. The focus now shifts to Trump's appearance at the World Economic Forum in Davos. Long-dated Treasury yields surged to their highest levels since late September amid the risk-off sentiment.

data.title: Dr. David Thrasher recognized as a healthcare leader, Eli Lilly to invest $6 billion in manufacturing facility in Alabama.
data.description: In a recent development, Alabama disclosed its largest-ever investment with pharmaceutical giant Eli Lilly. The company, valued as the world's most valuable in the field, is set to construct a cutting-edge manufacturing plant worth $6 billion in Huntsville. This significant endeavor marks a pivotal moment for the state's economic landscape.

data.title: Shrub Oak Intl School partners with GlamourGals' Mahopac Chapter for senior outreach at Kohler Senior Center.
data.description: In an effort to enhance community involvement, Shrub Oak International School in Mohegan Lake, New York, announced on January 20, 2026, its partnership with GlamourGals' Mahopac Chapter for senior outreach initiatives. This collaboration includes scheduled visits to the Kohler Senior Center in Mahopac, NY, among other locations. The school aims to increase its engagement with seniors and foster intergenerational connections through these efforts.

data.title: VPN.com CEO calls on ICANN to review ccTLD policy after censorship; urges reconsideration of gov't involvement.
data.description: Michael Gargiulo, CEO of VPN.com Domain Advisor, has urged ICANN to reconsider its policy on country code top-level domains (ccTLDs) amid government interventions on the Internet. The message was conveyed on Jan 20, 2026 in Atlanta and Washington.

data.title: Harvest Partners promotes 3 partners to Private Equity Investment Committee, along with multiple other promotions.
data.description: On January 20, 2026, Harvest Partners, LP revealed that Partners Steve Fessler, Jim Mitchel, and Nick Romano have been added to the Private Equity Investment Committee. The Firm also disclosed multiple promotions within the Private Equity department.

data.title: CFTC Chairman Selig launches "Future-Proof" initiative to rewrite crypto regs, shifting from enforcement to rule-writing. Updating current regulations for blockchain assets and AI trading to keep U.S. companies from going offshore.
data.description: The chairman of the CFTC, Michael S. Selig, has launched the "Future-Proof" initiative aimed at reworking crypto regulations in the United States. This initiative intends to put an end to enforcement-based policymaking that has driven American companies to operate offshore. Selig announced on Tuesday that the CFTC will transition from relying on enforcement actions to establish crypto policies to instead creating formal rules through public notice-and-comment procedures. The significance lies in the permanency of formal rules, making them harder for future administrations to reverse. In contrast, the Biden-era agencies relied on guidance documents and enforcement actions, which were promptly overturned by Trump regulators. Selig expressed in an op-ed for The Washington Post that they aim to cease regulation through enforcement and foster the growth of new markets domestically with tailored rules. The CFTC will undertake a comprehensive review of all current regulations tailored for agricultural futures contracts in order to update them for blockchain assets, AI-based trading, and prediction markets. The U.S. witnessed an exodus of crypto companies due to these stringent regulations.

data.title: SENS appoints Igor Stamenkovic as CEO, signaling transition for power solutions. LONGMONT, Colo., Jan. 20, 2026 PRNewswire - Leaders in mission-critical power solutions announced the change.
data.description: A leadership transition has been announced at Stored Energy Systems (SENS), a prominent provider of power solutions for critical infrastructure. The appointment of Igor Stamenkovic was revealed on Jan. 20, 2026, during the POWERGEN event in LONGMONT, Colo. This move is part of SENS' ongoing development towards integrated systems, emphasizing their commitment to advancing in the industry.
